verdict.headline: Do not transact — this wallet is on OFAC SDN and linked to the DPRK Lazarus Group.
verdict.reasoning: The sanctions check returned a definitive match against three programs: OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, and DPRK, which is a hard veto regardless of any other signal. On-chain history shows a substantial ETH balance (~101.8 ETH), but balance alone cannot offset a confirmed sanctions hit. Labels and web sentiment endpoints returned only API metadata (no actual label or sentiment data), so they provide no compensating positive evidence. Per policy, a sanctions match forces safe=false, verdict=do_not_transact, with high confidence.

findings.finding: Confirmed sanctions match on OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, and DPRK programs — transacting may violate sanctions law.
Label provider returned only API metadata; no usable labels retrieved for this address.
Active wallet holding ~101.80 ETH; non-trivial balance but does not mitigate sanctions risk.
Web sentiment endpoint returned only API metadata; no specific news or scam reports retrieved.

services.rationale: Only candidate with a confirmed 0% failure rate AND inputInfoCompleteness of 2/3, at the lowest available price ($0.001), making it the clear winner over untested peers and the 100%-failing index 5.

The only labels candidate with a confirmed 0% failure rate; despite all candidates sharing the same 1/3 completeness, proven real-world performance is the decisive signal, and its $0.005 price is competitive.

Confirmed 0% failure rate is the decisive signal over all untested alternatives and the 100%-failing index 1; lowest price ($0.001) further reinforces the pick despite 1/3 completeness shared across candidates.
